identity.title: One Tap Away
identity.styleSentence: A mid-tempo (96 BPM) A# minor track blending organic drums and brass with atmospheric synths to create a moody, late-night vibe that critiques modern social connectivity.

lyrics.text:
[singer A]
Yeah
one tap away
but somehow everybody far now
uh
check it

Back-back when the screen made the room glow
I thought the whole world would move close
old names popping up like whoa though
middle school faces in a new post

I thought we’d talk-talk, really get honest
drop the mask, no fake persona
find your tribe, find your people
make the whole cold world feel warmer

Ads in the cracks, brands in the chat
everybody got stats, everybody wants claps
everybody got reach but don’t reach back
how we got this close and lost contact?

[transition]

[singer B]
You can see me in a second
You can call me any day
You can love me in private
But scroll right past my name
Funny how we got connected
Then forgot how to stay
You're right there in my pocket
But you feel a world away

One tap away (one tap away)
From a real conversation but we don't say a thing
We're one tap away
From "I see you keep going" from "I'm proud of your dream"
Save your likes for the strangers
Save your hearts for the stars
But the ones who really know you act like loving you is hard
One tap away
So why do I feel alone in a room full of names?

Tap, tap, but you don't reach back
Heart, heart, but it don't feel like that
Close, close, close, but we don't connect
One tap away and we still forget

Click, click, click, everybody got a new rule
Don't hype friends that ain't cool, cool
Save that love for the big names
Blue checks, big chains, big views
Then if a stranger blow up overnight
Everybody swear they saw the light
Same ones quiet when you need a fire
Now they in the comments like "that's my guy"

[transition]

[singer A]
We one tap away
one tap away
from a real conversation
but we don’t say a thing

Save your likes for the strangers
save your hearts for the stars
but the ones who really know you
act like loving you is hard

Maybe I was young
thinking wires could heal us
thinking all this light
would finally reveal us

I’m not hard to find
I’m not far away
I’m still right here
one tap away

We one tap away
one tap away
so why do I feel alone
in a room full of names?

one tap away
and still so far
everybody online
nobody where you are

analysis.summary: The song critiques the paradoxical isolation inherent in digital connectivity, exploring how social media platforms create a physical proximity that feels like emotional distance.

sections.name: Bridge
sections.emotion: Existential dread in a crowded space

keywords.imageryPrompt: a solitary figure standing in the center of a vast, dark server room filled with blinking red lights, feeling physically cold
a glowing smartphone screen reflecting a person's tired eyes as their thumb hovers over the send button without touching it
silhouettes of people watching celebrities on large screens, giving thumbs up while ignoring each other in a dark room
a cracked mirror reflecting a distorted face behind an augmented reality filter that smooths out all imperfections instantly
two hands reaching towards each other across a chasm of static electricity and digital noise, unable to make contact
countless user profile names scrolling rapidly on an endless black screen like falling snow made of text that no one reads

dropMap.hint: cuts → blackout/cut-mode moments; risers → supernova build-ups (stemSense activeCut/activeRiser fire these automatically)
